name: Draconic Vigor
description: You trace the sigil of endurance — a coiled dragon — in the air before three creatures, breathing the Old Tongue word vorthûn (“unbroken”). Their blood quickens with ancestral resilience, their veins briefly shimmering with faint draconic light. Each target‘s maximum hit points increase by 5 for the duration, and their current hit points rise to match. This is not healing but bloodline fortification — the dragon within refusing to yield.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, each target’s hit point maximum increases by an additional 5 per spell level above 2nd.

name: Blood-Mask
description: You press a Fractured Flesh-Core to your sternum and whisper the Old Tongue phrase sheth-kar vey’n (“I am another”). Your bloodline sigil warps, rewriting your physical form. You may adopt draconic features — slit pupils, scaled forearms, taloned fingers — or reshape your entire appearance including height, build, and voice. You cannot change creature type, but you can mimic the appearance of any humanoid. Those who examine you closely may notice a faint shimmer where the Old Tongue words still crawl beneath your skin. Inquisitors call this spell Wyrm-Deceit, and those caught using it face the pyre.

name: Scale-Courier
description: You place a message against the Fractured Wild-Core and trace the sigil of the Winged Herald — a tiny dragon in flight. An aetherial construct takes form: a fist-sized draconic wisp with parchment wings and ember eyes. You whisper your destination and message; the Scale-Courier flits away at impossible speed, finding its target by blood-scent across any distance on the same plane. Upon arrival it speaks your words in your voice, then dissolves into harmless sparks. If the target’s bloodline is suppressed, dormant, or severed, the courier circles confusedly and returns.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the courier’s flight speed increases, allowing it to reach targets on adjacent planes by 5th level.

name: Aether-Seal
description: You press a Fractured Law-Core against a door, chest, or portal and speak the Old Tongue command keth-varôs (“none shall pass”). The core embeds itself into the surface, crackling with a lattice of draconic sigils. The object becomes magically locked; the DC to pick or break it increases by 10. Only those whose blood resonates with the sigil pattern you designate — or those whose purity exceeds yours — may open it without shattering the seal. A shattered seal screams, audible to any dragon-blood within 300 feet.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the DC to bypass the seal increases by 2 per spell level above 2nd.

name: Dragon-Eye Reading
description: You scatter the dust of a Fractured Wild-Core over a polished surface — still water, obsidian, or blood — and intone the Old Tongue query-phrase veyr’an sheth’kar? (“what comes?”). The dust writhes, forming the eye of a distant dragon ancestor that peers through time’s veil. You receive an omen about a specific course of action planned within 30 minutes: Weal (fortune), Woe (danger), Weal and Woe (mixed), or Nothing (fate is silent). Each casting after the first before a long rest beckons the Eye deeper; the Spell Safety DC increases by +3 per repeated casting. The dragon ancestors do not appreciate being pestered.

name: Drake-Hide
description: You trace the sigil of the Iron-Scaled Drake across a willing creature’s skin and speak the Old Tongue word vrak’sul (“armored flesh”). The target’s skin hardens into a lattice of semi-translucent scales, shimmering faintly with their bloodline’s color — crimson for fire lines, silver for storm lines, black for void lines. Their AC cannot drop below 16 for the duration. The transformation is visible to others; Inquisitors recognize it as a bloodline manifestation and will demand the caster’s certification papers.

name: Sense-Theft
description: You crush a Fractured Void-Core between thumb and forefinger while hissing the Old Tongue curse vel’shath mir (“see nothing”). Black mist coils from your hand and streaks toward a creature’s face. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be blinded or deafened (your choice) for the duration. The effect manifests as a writhing film of void-stuff over their eyes or ears — visible to all. A creature afflicted by this spell sees not darkness but the yawning emptiness between stars, which many describe as infinitely worse.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature per spell level above 2nd.

name: Aether-Shimmer
description: You draw the Fractured Shadow-Core across your chest in a zigzag pattern and exhale the Old Tongue word vish’tal (“between”). Your bloodline sigil stutters, projecting you fractionally out of phase with the material world. Your image becomes blurred, shifting and wavering as though seen through heat haze. Attack rolls against you have disadvantage unless the attacker can see through illusions. The effect is anchored to your bloodline — suppressing your purity suppresses the shimmer. Sovereigns use this to walk through crowds unnoticed; Inquisitors have learned to watch for the telltale afterimage.

name: Sovereign’s Brand
description: You slam a Fractured Law-Core against the grip of your weapon and roar the Old Tongue oath keth-ragnar vorn! (“by the First Flame, be marked!”). Draconic fire wreathes your weapon, inscribing your bloodline sigil along the blade in searing light. The next time you strike a creature, the sigil transfers to their flesh, burning bright as a brand. For the duration, the target sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius, cannot become invisible, and any attack you make against them deals an additional 2d6 radiant damage. If the target dies while branded, the sigil detonates in a harmless flash visible to dragon-blood within 500 feet — a declaration of kill-right.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the initial and ongoing radiant damage increases by 1d6 per spell level above 2nd.

name: Still the Blood
description: You speak the Old Tongue lullaby sul-nir vorthûn (“calm, unbroken”) while weaving your bloodline sigil in slow, soothing arcs. Each creature of your choice within a 20-foot-radius sphere must make a Charisma saving throw. On failure, their draconic blood — whether dormant or active — is temporarily placated, suppressing extreme emotions. A creature so affected becomes indifferent toward creatures it was hostile toward, and cannot take violent actions. The effect ends if the target is attacked or harmed. This spell is banned by the Inquisition in most Citadel precincts; it is considered bloodline coercion.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the radius increases by 10 feet per spell level above 2nd.

name: Eternal Ember
description: You press a Fractured Fire-Core to an object — a torch, a lantern, a brazier — and speak the Old Tongue phrase ignar’sheth vor (“fire that never dies”). The core fuses into the object, which ignites with a warm, steady flame that produces no heat unless you will it, consumes no fuel, and cannot be extinguished by wind, water, or smothering. The light is the exact color of your bloodline’s draconic fire — gold for Law, crimson for Cinder, silver for Storm. The flame can be covered to hide the light, but it never truly dies. Many Sump-dwellers trade dearly for an Eternal Ember; it is the only light that asks nothing in return.

name: Void-Wing Shroud
description: You shatter a Fractured Void-Core above your head and spread your arms wide, invoking the Old Tongue phrase vel’sul karôs (“the deep claims this place”). The shards expand into a 15-foot-radius sphere of absolute, magical darkness. No light — mundane or magical — can illuminate the area, and creatures within are effectively blinded. The darkness does not simply obscure light; it eats it, swallowing torchlight and spells alike. Those inside report hearing distant wingbeats. This is the inherited magic of Void-blooded dragons, and it terrifies even the Inquisition.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the radius increases by 5 feet per spell level above 2nd.

name: Hunt-Sight
description: You press a Fractured Shadow-Core to a willing creature’s closed eyes and whisper the Old Tongue word veyr’kar (“true sight”). The core dissolves, its essence seeping into the target’s irises, which flicker with a faint amber glow for the duration. The target gains darkvision out to 60 feet — but unlike mundane night-sight, this is draconic perception, allowing them to see heat signatures in monochrome red. To those without the spell active, the target’s eyes appear to have vertical slit pupils for the duration. Inquisitors trained to spot this call it “wyrm-eye” and consider it probable cause.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the range increases by 30 feet per spell level above 2nd.

name: Mind-Peel
description: You touch the Fractured Mind-Core to your temple and speak the Old Tongue phrase sheth’mir vorn (“I read the unwritten”). A cold pulse spreads through your skull as your bloodline attunes to the surface thoughts of those around you. For the duration, you can perceive the shallow mental impressions of any creature within 30 feet, even if you cannot see them. You may also focus on one creature to probe deeper, forcing a Wisdom saving throw; on failure, you pluck a desire, fear, or secret from their mind. The target feels a distinct warmth behind their eyes — the dragon, looking. Those with higher purity resist more easily, their bloodline instinctively guarding their mind.

name: Blood-Surge
description: You clutch a Fractured Flesh-Core and draw your bloodline sigil directly onto the target’s skin with its edge, chanting the Old Tongue litany of the six aspects: vrak’sul, mir’thal, vor’nir, sheth’kar, sul’dan, keth’ragnar. The target’s blood surges with ancestral power, magnifying one of six aspects — draconic might, feline grace, ursine constitution, serpentine cunning, owlish wisdom, or regal presence. They gain advantage on ability checks using the chosen attribute. The sigil glows visibly through clothing for the duration, a pulsing tattoo of power and danger.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature per spell level above 2nd.

name: Titan-Blood
description: You speak the Old Tongue words of scale — gran’sul (“grow”) or mir’sul (“shrink”) — while tracing a spiraling sigil toward your target with a Fractured Flesh-Core. The target’s body responds to the blood-command, their skeleton groaning or compressing as the ancient proportions of the Great Dragons — who were as large as mountains and as small as embers — temporarily overwrite their form. A creature or object you enlarge doubles in all dimensions and multiplies its weight by eight; one you reduce halves in dimensions and divides weight by eight. Those who survive this spell often speak of phantom limb pains for weeks.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the size multiplier increases — at 4th level a creature can grow to Gargantuan or shrink to Fine.

name: Summon Blood-Mount
description: You draw a wide circle in the ground with a Fractured Flesh-Core, tracing the sigil of the Loyal Drake, and cut your palm — letting a drop of blood fall into the circle’s center. The blood steams and expands, forming a loyal steed woven from Aether and your own bloodline memory: a horse-sized drake with leathery wings (flightless unless you have 50%+ purity), obsidian scales, and eyes that match your own. The Blood-Mount serves you absolutely, understands Old Tongue commands, and fights alongside you if needed. It is not a true creature but a blood-manifestation; when it dies, it dissolves into a pool of warm blood that smells faintly of your ancestry.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the Blood-Mount gains +1 AC and +10 hit points per spell level above 2nd.

name: Aether-Sight
description: You hold a Fractured Wild-Core before one eye like a lens and speak the Old Tongue word veyr’snâr (“see the hidden”). The core pulses once, discharging a wave of perception that ripples through the area. For an instant, any mechanical trap, alchemical snare, or magical ward within range glimmers with an aether-blue outline visible only to you — the core revealing where the weave has been disturbed. This spell does not detect natural hazards, but it reveals sigil-locked doors, Inquisitor alarm wards, and buried Aether-Core mines. The flash is brief; you must commit what you see to memory or lose it.

name: Cinder-Blade
description: You break a Fractured Fire-Core in your grip and roar the Old Tongue blade-name ignar’shek vor (“flame fang, come”). The core’s shards ignite and elongate into a scimitar-shaped blade of roaring draconic fire in your hand. The Cinder-Blade sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius, dim light for 20 feet, and deals 3d6 fire damage on a hit. It leaves no wound — only ash and cauterized flesh. You can use your spellcasting ability instead of Strength for attack and damage rolls. The blade answers to your bloodline alone; if another attempts to wield it, the fire turns on them.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 per two spell levels above 2nd.

name: Rolling Cinder
description: You hurl a Fractured Fire-Core to an unoccupied space on the ground and call the Old Tongue phrase ignar’dan vor (“dragon’s eye, wake”). The core erupts into a 5-foot-diameter sphere of churning draconic fire — a miniature sun with a vertical slit-pupil at its center. The Rolling Cinder sheds bright light for 20 feet and dim light for 40. As a bonus action, you can command it to roll up to 30 feet; it ignites flammable objects and ramming into a creature deals 2d6 fire damage (Dexterity save for half). Those who stare into its pupil swear they see something staring back.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 per spell level above 2nd.

name: Death-Stilling
description: You rest a Fractured Death-Core on a corpse’s forehead and hum the Old Tongue dirge sul’nir mir’sheth (“rest, unborn”). The core’s cold seeps into the body, halting decay, preventing reanimation, and extending the window for spells like Death-Defy (Revivify) by the full 10 days. The corpse’s blood — regardless of purity — congeals into a thin crystalline layer just beneath the skin, visible as a faint shimmer. In Solis-Grave culture, this is a final dignity: to be preserved not by prayer but by blood-right. The Inquisition considers it unsanctioned if performed by anyone but an Ordained.

name: Wing-Gust
description: You sweep your arms forward as if beating wings and exhale the Old Tongue word vayr’shul (“blow”) through a Fractured Storm-Core held to your lips. A line of howling draconic wind 60 feet long and 10 feet wide blasts from you for the duration. Creatures in the line must make a Strength saving throw or be pushed 15 feet. The wind disperses gas, vapor, and fog, and extinguishes unprotected flames. For the duration, you can change the direction of the line as a bonus action. Archons describe this spell as “borrowing the wings of your great-grandfather.”
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the line’s length increases by 10 feet per spell level above 2nd.

name: Forge-Touch
description: You point a Fractured Fire-Core at a metal object — a sword, a suit of armor, an Inquisitor’s helm — and speak the Old Tongue word for “smith”: gra’nôk. The metal instantly heats to a cherry-red glow, dealing 2d8 fire damage to any creature in physical contact with it at the moment of casting. If the creature is holding or wearing the heated object and does not drop or doff it, it takes the damage again at the end of each of your subsequent turns as a bonus action. Aether-Smiths use this spell as both a forging tool and a weapon; it can soften metal to be reshaped or turn a warrior’s own plate into a death trap.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 per spell level above 2nd.

name: Wyrm’s Grasp
description: You extend your hand, fingers curled like talons, and speak the Old Tongue command keth’shath vorn (“you are held”) while fixing a Fractured Law-Core on your target. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be paralyzed as their bloodline — dormant or active — is overridden by a stronger draconic command. The magic does not freeze their muscles; it commands their blood to stillness, pooling icy and disobedient in their veins. While paralyzed, the target is conscious and aware, their eyes tracking movement with a terror known only to prey pinned by a dragon’s gaze.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature per spell level above 2nd. Each target must be within 30 feet of the others.

name: Aether-Veil
description: You crush a Fractured Shadow-Core over a creature’s head and whisper the Old Tongue word vish’tal mir (“between seen”). The core’s shadow-stuff cascades over them, bending light around their form. They become invisible until they attack or cast a spell. The invisibility is not perfect — a creature with a strong bloodline (purity 50%+) can make a Wisdom (Perception) check as an action to detect a faint shimmer, like heat rising from sun-baked stone. Inquisitors train their hounds to scent the lingering Shadow-Core residue.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature per spell level above 2nd.

name: Wyrm’s Unlocking
description: You tap a Fractured Wild-Core against any solid surface and speak the Old Tongue phrase vorn’shek keth? (“barrier, what barrier?”). A deep, resonant click echoes from the targeted lock, manacle, or door — the sound of ancestral dragon authority overriding mortal craftsmanship. Any non-magical lock opens; any arcane lock (including Aether-Seal) is suppressed for 10 minutes. The unlocking is accompanied by a faint draconic roar, audible up to 300 feet. This is the most frequently-prosecuted spell in Citadel courts; possession of a Wild-Core and intent to unlock is enough for conviction.

name: Blood-Cleanse
description: You press a Fractured Law-Core to the afflicted creature and speak the Old Tongue purification: sul’vorn sheth’nar (“let the blood remember itself”). The core pulses with soft gold light as it forces the target’s bloodline to reject foreign contamination. This ends one of the following: blinded, deafened, paralyzed, or poisoned condition, or one disease. It does not remove curses — those are embedded deeper than blood. The target experiences a brief, vivid memory of a dragon ancestor, as if the blood itself is reminding them what they are.

name: Dragon-Lift
description: You sweep a Fractured Storm-Core upward while speaking the Old Tongue word vayr’sul (“rise”). A column of shimmering aether envelops one willing creature, and they feel the phantom weight of draconic wings settle across their shoulders. They ascend vertically up to 20 feet and hover there, able to move only by pushing or pulling along fixed surfaces. The magic does not grant flight — it merely denies gravity — but to the unblooded, it looks like ascension. Inquisitors have a standing order: any non-Ordained seen levitating is to be detained or shot down.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature per spell level above 2nd.

name: Blood-Seek
description: You hold a Fractured Wild-Core in your open palm and speak the name or description of an object, followed by the Old Tongue phrase veyr’shek karôs? (“where lies the thing?”). The core trembles, then tugs your hand like a compass needle, guiding you toward the nearest matching object within 1,000 feet that you have seen before. The pull is subtle — a warmth in the right direction — and grows stronger as you approach. This is not true divination but bloodline resonance: your ancestors hoarded treasures; they never forgot where a single coin was buried.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the range increases by 500 feet per spell level above 2nd.

name: Whispering Sigil
description: You trace your bloodline sigil onto a surface with a Fractured Mind-Core and speak the Old Tongue phrase mir’shek vorn (“remember these words”) followed by a message of up to 25 words. The core’s residue sinks into the material, invisible until triggered. You set the trigger condition — someone touches the sigil, speaks a certain name, enters a bloodline into the room. When triggered, a spectral dragon mouth manifests over the sigil and delivers your message in your voice. The mouth then fades, and the sigil goes dormant for 10 minutes before resetting. Spies call these “dragon’s teeth” — scattered everywhere, waiting to bite.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the message length doubles, and you can specify that the sigil only activates for creatures of a minimum purity threshold.

name: Splintered Self
description: You spin a Fractured Shadow-Core around yourself in a blur while whispering the Old Tongue phrase sheth’vish vorn mir (“I am many, see none”). Three illusory duplicates splinter from your form — each a perfect copy, shifting and overlapping so that an attacker cannot distinguish the real from the false. Each duplicate has its own bloodline sigil flickering faintly; only yours burns true. When an attack would hit you, roll a d20; on a 6+ for three duplicates, 8+ for two, or 11+ for one, the attack hits a duplicate instead, destroying it. Creatures with high purity (75%+) can see through the ruse more easily.

name: Smoke-Shift
description: You crush a Fractured Shadow-Core in your fist and utter the Old Tongue word vish’tal vorn (“between here”). Your body dissolves into a plume of silver-black smoke smelling faintly of your draconic heritage — sulfur for red lines, ozone for storm lines, cold nothing for void lines. You reappear up to 30 feet away in an unoccupied space you can see, the smoke coalescing back into form. In the instant of transit, you exist neither here nor there, and cannot be targeted or affected. This is the most-practiced escape spell among Archons; more have survived Inquisitor raids thanks to Smoke-Shift than any other magic.

name: Argent Beam
description: You hold a Fractured Light-Core to the sky and speak the Old Tongue word selûn’vayr (“moonlight, descend”). A pillar of silver-white radiance 5 feet wide and 40 feet high descends from above, regardless of ceilings or barriers — it phases through solid matter to reach its designated point. The beam burns with the cold light of the pale moon, dealing 2d10 radiant damage to any creature that enters it for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there (Constitution save for half). Shapechangers struck by the beam are forced into their natural form. The light is the inherited echo of a Silver Dragon who once placed the moon in the sky, or so the Lore-Keepers sing.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d10 per spell level above 2nd.

name: Ghost-Scale
description: You press a Fractured Shadow-Core to your heart and hum the Old Tongue lullaby of the unseen hunter: vish’kar sul’dan (“unseen, patient, still”). A veil of shadow and silence settles over you and up to five creatures you choose within 30 feet. For the duration, you leave no tracks, make no sound, and gain a +10 bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) checks. The magic works by projecting a faint draconic camouflage — the inherited talent of Shadow Wyrmlings who hunted by erasing their own presence. To the mundane eye, you simply aren’t there. A creature with 75%+ purity can make a Wisdom (Perception) check as an action to notice the faint shimmer.

name: Healing Chant
description: You kneel and place a Fractured Light-Core at the center of a circle of up to six creatures, then begin the Old Tongue healing chant: sul’vorn sheth’mir, vrak’sul vorthûn (“let blood remember wholeness, let flesh be unbroken”). Over ten minutes, the core pulses with steady, rhythmic light — like a heartbeat — and each creature in the circle regains 2d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier hit points. This is not prayer but blood-song, the same draconic vocalization a mother dragon uses to heal her clutch. The Inquisition permits Ordained to cast this; anyone else is practicing “unsanctioned blood-healing.”
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the healing increases by 1d8 per spell level above 2nd.

name: Venom Ward
description: You press a Fractured Flesh-Core to a creature’s chest and speak the Old Tongue word vorn’ven sul (“poison, be still”). The core pulses once, and a faint lattice of scales appears across the target’s skin — visible only at certain angles — before fading. For the duration, the target has resistance to poison damage and advantage on saving throws against being poisoned. If they were already poisoned, the condition is neutralized for the duration. The magic works by commanding the target’s blood to recognize venom as foreign and reject it, the same instinct that made ancient dragons immune to every serpent’s bite.

name: Sapping Bolt
description: You point a Fractured Death-Core at a creature and speak the Old Tongue curse vrak’sul mir (“flesh, weaken”). A black beam streaked with marrow-white light leaps from the core to the target, who must make a Constitution saving throw. On failure, the target’s muscles wither as their bloodline is momentarily drained — they deal only half damage with weapon attacks using Strength for the duration. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on success. The beam leaves no visible wound, but the target’s veins darken visibly beneath the skin for the duration.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the target has disadvantage on the initial saving throw if you cast this spell using a spell slot one level higher than the target’s maximum spell slot level.

name: Aether-Pocket
description: You trace a spiraling sigil on a length of rope with a Fractured Wild-Core and speak the Old Tongue phrase karôs vish’tal (“place between”). The rope rises, its top end anchoring to an invisible point in the air — a pocket of folded space, a dragon’s trick of hoarding a den where there should be none. Up to eight creatures can climb the rope into the extradimensional space, which hangs suspended. The entrance is invisible from outside. From inside, the entrance appears as a faint shimmer. When the spell ends, creatures inside fall. This was originally a roost-spell used by Dragon-Lords to sleep safely above their treasure hoards.

name: Tri-Fire Bolt
description: You snap a Fractured Fire-Core into three shards and hurl them while snarling the Old Tongue phrase ignar’shek vor, ignar’shek vor, ignar’shek vor! (“flame fang, come — three times!”). Each shard ignites mid-flight into a brilliant bolt of draconic fire, streaking toward targets you designate. Make a ranged spell attack for each bolt, dealing 2d6 fire damage on a hit. The bolts can target a single creature or multiple. The fire burns with your bloodline’s color — crimson, gold, silver, or void-black. Flammable objects ignite on hit. Experienced Archons can curve the bolts around cover with a slight wrist-twist.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, you create one additional bolt per spell level above 2nd.

name: Unveil the Hidden
description: You hold a Fractured Light-Core before your eyes and speak the Old Tongue word veyr’shek vish’tal (“see the between”). Your irises flicker silver as the core’s essence attunes your sight to the aetheric spectrum. For the duration, you see invisible creatures and objects as if they were faintly glowing, and you perceive ethereal beings within 120 feet. Any creature hiding its bloodline (Dormancy) appears to you with their true purity visible as a faint aura — red for active, grey for suppressed, black for severed. The Inquisition considers casting this in Citadel precincts without authorization a third-degree offense.

name: Sonic Rupture
description: You crush a Fractured Storm-Core against a hard surface and scream the Old Tongue roar vayr’kath vorn! (“sky-break!”). A thunderous detonation erupts from a point you choose within range, rolling outward in a 10-foot-radius sphere. Each creature in the area must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 3d8 thunder damage on failure or half on success. Creatures made of stone, crystal, or metal have disadvantage. The shockwave is visibly draconic — witnesses report seeing a spectral dragon’s maw open at the point of detonation. Unsecured objects within the area are flung 10 feet.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 per spell level above 2nd.

name: Sound-Death
description: You hold a Fractured Void-Core to your lips and breathe the Old Tongue command sul’vayr mir (“silence, now”). A 20-foot-radius sphere of absolute silence manifests at a point you choose. No sound can be created within or pass through the sphere; creatures inside are effectively deafened and immune to thunder damage. The sphere is not visible, but those who enter it feel an oppressive weight — the same suffocating quiet of the Void between stars, where even dragons dare not roar. Verbal spell components cannot be spoken within the sphere.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the radius increases by 5 feet per spell level above 2nd.

name: Aether-Blade
description: You toss a Fractured Law-Core into the air and speak the Old Tongue blade-rite: shek’vorn keth’nar (“blade of judgment, attend”). The core hangs midair and unfolds into a floating spectral weapon shaped like a dragon’s talon, a fang, or a scaled fist — depending on your bloodline. As a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can move the blade up to 20 feet and make a melee spell attack against a creature within 5 feet of it, dealing 1d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier force damage. The blade is made of pure aetheric law and bypasses all damage resistance.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 per two spell levels above 2nd.

name: Dragon’s Command
description: You fix a Fractured Mind-Core on a creature and speak a short, reasonable-sounding command in the Old Tongue — sheth’kar vorn (“you will do this”). The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On failure, they follow the command to the best of their ability for the duration. The command must sound reasonable; asking the target to harm themselves or obviously walk into a deathtrap automatically negates the spell. This is not charm — it is draconic authority asserting itself over another’s bloodline. Lesser-blooded creatures (purity below 25%) make the save with disadvantage. The Inquisition considers this a form of bloodline domination and prosecutes it as attempted slavery.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the duration increases — 24 hours at 5th level, 30 days at 7th level, 1 year at 9th level.

name: Blood-Oath Link
description: You and a willing creature each grasp one half of a Fractured Law-Core, pull it apart, and speak the Old Tongue vow in unison: keth’vorn vrak’sul, mir’shek vorn’sul (“my blood is yours, your wound is mine”). The core halves embed in each of your chests, glowing faintly through clothing. For the duration, the target gains +1 to AC and saving throws, and has resistance to all damage. However, whenever the target takes damage, you take the same amount as psychic feedback. The link is visible — a thin, glowing filament of aetheric light connects the two core halves. Breaking the link requires severing the filament with a dispel or by moving more than 100 feet apart. Oath-Sworn paladins use this to protect their charges; the ritual is the foundation of their order.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the AC and saving throw bonus increases by +1 per two spell levels above 2nd.

name: Silk-Grasp
description: You throw a Fractured Wild-Core at a point and speak the Old Tongue phrase shek’var vorn’shath (“threads, bind them”). The core bursts into a 20-foot cube of sticky, semi-translucent webbing — not spider silk but crystallized aether-threads that smell faintly of draconic musk. The webs anchor to all surfaces and are difficult terrain. Each creature that starts its turn in the webs or enters them must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be restrained. A creature can use its action to make a Strength check against your spell save DC to break free. The webs are flammable; any fire damage destroys a 5-foot cube and deals 2d4 fire damage to creatures within.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the cube size increases by 5 feet per spell level above 2nd.

name: Verity Circle
description: You draw a 15-foot-radius circle on the ground with a Fractured Law-Core and intone the Old Tongue truth-invocation: vorn’shek sheth’mir, vel’sul mir’shek (“speak true, or the void sees”). The circle’s boundary ignites with pale draconic flame. Any creature that enters or starts its turn in the circle must make a Charisma saving throw. On failure, they cannot knowingly speak a lie while within the circle. They may refuse to answer, or speak in half-truths, but outright falsehoods catch in their throat like swallowed glass. An affected creature is aware of the compulsion. This spell is the centerpiece of Citadel judicial proceedings — all testimony before an Ordained magistrate is given within a Verity Circle.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the duration increases to 1 hour at 4th level, 24 hours at 7th level, and the target cannot refuse to answer direct questions at 9th level.

name: Crimson Lash
description: You slice your palm with a Fractured Blood-Core and speak the Old Tongue curse vrak’sul shek’vorn (“my blood, become my blade”). The blood that wells from the wound does not fall — it elongates into a 10-foot crimson lash that floats beside you, crackling with your own life force. As a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can strike a creature within 10 feet. Make a melee spell attack; on a hit, the target takes 2d6 necrotic damage, and you regain hit points equal to half the damage dealt. Each time you deal damage with the lash, you take 1d4 necrotic damage that cannot be reduced — the hemocraft tax. The lash leaves whip-like scars that never fully fade, recognized by other Crimson Oaths as a blood-sibling’s mark.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the lash’s reach increases by 5 feet and the damage increases by 1d6 per spell level above 2nd.

name: Aether-Bolt Gadget
description: You slot a Fractured Storm-Core into a handheld device — a gauntlet-mounted launcher, a modified crossbow, or a wand of coiled copper wire — and pull the trigger while incanting the Old Tongue ignition phrase vayr’shek gran’sul (“lightning, grow”). The gadget crackles, vibrates violently, and discharges a 60-foot line of arcing aetheric lightning. Each creature in the line must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d6 lightning damage on failure or half on success. The device smokes and hisses after firing; the Aether-Core is not consumed unless the spell fizzles. Aether-Smiths are constantly iterating on the design, with some models featuring rifled barrels, focusing lenses, or safety catches that rarely work.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 per spell level above 2nd.

name: Dragon’s Authority
description: You raise a Fractured Law-Core like a scepter and speak a single Old Tongue command — keth! (stop), sheth! (kneel), vayr! (flee), or mir! (speak) — and your bloodline asserts itself over another’s. Choose a creature within range; it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or obey the command on its next turn. Creatures with lower purity than you make the save with disadvantage. Creatures with higher purity are immune. This is the raw power of a Dragon-Heir — not learned magic but inherited authority. A Sovereign child could command a room of Blanks before they could walk. The Inquisition cannot prosecute this spell because, by law, a Sovereign IS the law.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature per spell level above 2nd. All targets must be within 30 feet of each other.

name: Oathbound Strike
description: You recite the first line of your sworn oath in Old Tongue and crack a Fractured Law-Core against your breastplate, where it shatters in a burst of pale flame that engulfs your weapon. For the duration, your weapon attacks deal an additional 1d6 radiant damage, and when you strike a creature, a spectral chain of golden light briefly tethers them to you. The target cannot take the Disengage action against you and must make a Wisdom saving throw each time it tries to move more than 30 feet away from you or be pulled back to the edge of that range. This is the mark of an Oath-Sworn — you have declared this enemy under judgment, and there is nowhere they can flee that your oath will not find them.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the radiant damage increases by 1d6 per two spell levels above 2nd.

name: Predator’s Mark
description: You prick your finger on the edge of a Fractured Flesh-Core and smear your blood across your eyelids while whispering the Old Tongue hunter’s vow: veyr’shath vorn’sul (“my eyes, your trail”). Choose a creature you can see; for the duration, you always know the direction and approximate distance to that creature as long as it is on the same plane. You have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) and Wisdom (Perception) checks related to tracking or finding the marked creature, and your weapon attacks against it score a critical hit on a roll of 19-20. Striders call this “painting the prey” — once marked, no river, no storm, no Aether-Shroud can wash the scent from their blood.
higher_level: At 3rd level or higher, the critical hit range increases to 18-20 at 5th level.

name: Raise the Hollow
description: You kneel before a pile of bones or a corpse and press an Intact Death-Core into its chest cavity, reciting the Old Tongue rite of the Hollow King: vrak’sul mir’sheth, vorn’sul vel’shath (“dead flesh, rise hollow”). The core sinks into the remains and reignites them with a pale, cold flame — not life, but animation. You create a skeleton or zombie under your control for 24 hours. The Hollow has no soul, no bloodline, no will; it is a shell animated by the Aether-Core within, obeying your verbal commands. When destroyed or the duration ends, the core shatters and the remains collapse. Only the most desperate or most corrupted casters raise the Hollow. The Inquisition burns anyone caught doing so — caster and Hollow together.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, you create or reassert control over one additional Hollow per spell level above 3rd.

name: Vital Radiance
description: You press an Intact Light-Core over your heart and breathe the Old Tongue phrase sul’vorn vrak’nir (“life, flow outward”). The core dissolves into warm golden light radiating from your chest in a 30-foot aura. On each of your turns as a bonus action, you can direct the radiance to heal one creature within the aura (including yourself) for 2d6 hit points. The light carries the faint scent of your draconic ancestry — smoke for fire lines, petrichor for storm lines, old stone for earth. This spell was developed by Ordained combat medics during the Purge Wars, when triage required healing multiple wounded while holding a defensive line. The Inquisition licenses it only to certified battlefield clerics.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the healing increases by 1d6 per spell level above 3rd.

name: Dragon’s Beacon
description: You hold an Intact Light-Core aloft and roar the Old Tongue rally-cry vorthûn keth’ragnar! (“unbroken, the First Flame!”). The core erupts in a pillar of golden light 30 feet high, visible for miles. Choose up to six creatures within range; they are bathed in the beacon’s light and filled with ancestral resolve. For the duration, they have advantage on Wisdom saving throws and Death saving throws, and any healing they receive is maximized. The beacon is not subtle — every dragon-blood within 10 miles feels a faint warmth and knows the direction of the light. This spell is the Ordained’s battlefield standard; when the Dragon’s Beacon rises, even the dying fight on.

name: Wyrm’s Malediction
description: You touch a creature with an Intact Void-Core and whisper their name in Old Tongue, followed by the curse-formula: sheth’vel mir’sul vrak’nir (“may your blood betray you”). The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be cursed for the duration. You choose one affliction: disadvantage on an ability score’s checks and saves, disadvantage on attack rolls against you, the target must make a Wisdom save at the start of each turn or waste its action, or your attacks deal an extra 1d8 necrotic damage to them. A creature can only suffer one Wyrm’s Malediction at a time. The curse manifests as a faint black sigil, visible only to those with active purity, branded on the target’s shadow.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the duration increases — 10 minutes at 5th level, 1 hour at 7th level, and you may choose a second affliction at 9th level.

name: Phase-Step
description: You press an Intact Shadow-Core to your chest and speak the Old Tongue phrase vish’tal vish’tal vorn (“between between here”), repeating it until the words blur together. For the duration, at the end of each of your turns, roll a d20. On an 11 or higher, you vanish from the material plane and reappear in the Ethereal Plane until the start of your next turn, at which point you return in the same spot or the nearest unoccupied space. You can still see the material world as a grey haze, but you cannot affect or be affected by anything on it except force damage and effects that specifically reach into the Ethereal. This is the maddening gift of Void-blooded dragons, who learned to exist in two places at once.

name: Storm-Call
description: You raise an Intact Storm-Core to the sky and roar the Old Tongue invocation vayr’gran sul’dan vor! (“great storm, answer my blood!”). A roiling storm-cloud 60 feet high and 120 feet wide forms above you, crackling with your bloodline’s elemental color. On each of your turns as an action, you call down a bolt of draconic lightning to strike a point beneath the cloud, dealing 3d10 lightning damage to each creature within 5 feet (Dexterity save for half). If cast outdoors during a storm, the damage increases to 4d10. The cloud follows you, and you can call lightning even through ceilings and barriers. Striders call this “borrowing the sky’s fury.”
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d10 per spell level above 3rd.

name: Far-Sight
description: You sit cross-legged, place an Intact Mind-Core at the center of your forehead, and enter the Old Tongue trance veyr’sul karôs (“send my sight to that place”). Your consciousness detaches from your body and arrives at a familiar location or an obvious place within range — a dragon’s roost, a Sovereign’s throne room, the last campfire you slept beside. You see and hear as if you were there. You cannot interact, and your body remains senseless where you left it, vulnerable. Skilled casters learn to leave an Aether-Blade or a guardian construct to watch their physical form during Far-Sight. Inquisitor counter-intelligence agents are trained to detect the faint shimmer of a Far-Sight sensor.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the range doubles per spell level above 3rd.

name: Beast-Summoning
description: You hurl an Intact Flesh-Core to the ground and speak the Old Tongue summons vrak’nir gran’sul, vorn’sul karôs (“wild things, come to this place”). The core sinks into the earth and pulses, sending out a draconic call that only beasts can hear. 1d3 creatures of challenge rating 1 or lower, or one creature of challenge rating 2 or lower, answer the call — materializing from aether, drawn by the scent of dragon-blood. They are not tamed but instinctively loyal, obeying your verbal commands in Old Tongue. The creatures are temporary flesh-forms, not true animals; when the spell ends, they dissolve into smoke that smells faintly of your bloodline.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the CR of summoned creatures increases — CR 2 or lower for multiple, CR 3 for single at 5th level, scaling upward.

name: Nullify
casting_time: 1 reaction, which you take when you see a creature within 60 feet casting a spell
description: You snap an Intact Law-Core in two with a sharp gesture — no words, just the crack of the core and the assertion of your bloodline against another’s. The triggering spell, if it is 3rd level or lower, is nullified. If it is 4th level or higher, make an ability check using your spellcasting ability (DC 10 + the spell’s level); on success, the spell is nullified. The nullification manifests as a brief, visible collision of two draconic sigils in the air — yours vs. theirs — before both shatter. This is the ultimate expression of bloodline authority: “Your magic is lesser.” The core is not consumed on a successful nullification, but it is consumed if you fail the ability check.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, you automatically nullify spells of the same level as the slot used or lower without an ability check.

name: Blood-Sustain
description: You press an Intact Flesh-Core into the earth and speak the Old Tongue phrase vrak’sul gran’nir, sul’vorn sheth’mir (“flesh grow, blood sustain”). The ground trembles as the core’s essence transmutes soil and stone into food and water — 45 pounds of nutrient-dense, grey-scaled fruit and 30 gallons of clean, cool water. The fruit tastes faintly metallic and of your bloodline (Cinder-blooded create spicy fruit; Frost-blooded create cool, crisp fruit), but sustains a humanoid for a full day per pound. This is draconic survival magic, developed by Wild-Bloods who withdrew into the deep wilds during the Purge Wars. The Inquisition has never ruled on whether Blood-Sustain fruit is “blood-magic contraband” — perhaps because too many Citadel granaries are empty.

name: Dragon’s Radiance
description: You raise an Intact Light-Core overhead and speak the Old Tongue dawn-word ignar’selûn gran (“great dragon’s sun”). The core flares into a sphere of brilliant draconic radiance 60 feet in radius, shedding true sunlight for the duration. The light is centered on the core, which floats where you designate (or in your hand). Creatures with sunlight sensitivity are affected, and any darkness — magical or otherwise — within the sphere is suppressed. The light is the exact warmth and hue of the long-vanished Sun Dragon who once lit the world, or so the Ordained claim. They also claim that the sun itself was a dragon’s egg. Whether this is metaphor or truth has been debated for three centuries.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the radius increases by 30 feet per spell level above 3rd.

name: Spell-Shred
description: You hurl an Intact Law-Core at a creature, object, or area of magical effect and roar the Old Tongue negation: vel’shath sheth’kar! (“come undone!”). The core detonates in a silent, colorless shockwave that shreds the aetheric weave. Any spell of 3rd level or lower on the target ends. For spells of 4th level or higher, make an ability check with your spellcasting ability (DC 10 + the spell’s level) to end them. This is the universal solvent of magic — the assertion that no spell endures against a stronger bloodline. Aether-Smiths describe the process as “un-spelling” and have developed spectacles that tint the normally-invisible shockwave in colors keyed to spell schools.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, you automatically end the effects of spells of the same level as the slot used or lower without an ability check.

name: Dread-Presence
description: You crush an Intact Void-Core against your chest and release the draconic terror that slumbers in every bloodline. You don’t cast a spell — you unveil yourself. All creatures in a 30-foot cone must make a Wisdom saving throw or become frightened for the duration. While frightened, they must take the Dash action on each turn to move away from you by the safest route, unless there is nowhere to go. At the end of each turn, they may repeat the save. To the targets, you appear to briefly manifest aspects of your dragon ancestor — wings of shadow, eyes of molten gold, a voice that is yours and not yours. This is the closest most mortals will ever come to standing before a true dragon.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the cone extends by 10 feet per spell level above 3rd.

name: Cinder Burst
description: You hurl an Intact Fire-Core at a point within range and speak the Old Tongue detonation: ignar’gran sheth’kar! (“great fire, become!”). A bead of compressed draconic flame streaks from your hand and blooms into a 20-foot-radius sphere of roaring fire. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 fire damage on failure or half on success. The fire is not mundane — it is your bloodline’s ancestral flame, and it ignites flammable objects not by heat but by commanding their essence to burn. The explosion briefly forms the shape of a dragon’s skull before dissipating. Survivors often report the smell of their own singed bloodline for days afterward.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 per spell level above 3rd.

name: Wing-Gift
description: You trace the sigil of the Grand Flight across a creature’s shoulder blades with an Intact Storm-Core and speak the Old Tongue phrase vayr’sul vorn’shath (“sky, bear this one”). The core’s essence suffuses the target, manifesting as a pair of spectral draconic wings — translucent, shimmering, the color of their bloodline. The target gains a flying speed of 60 feet for the duration. The wings are not physical (the target’s clothing is not displaced), and they vanish if the target’s purity is suppressed. This is the most coveted gift a Dragon-Heir can bestow; to be Wing-Gifted is to be acknowledged as blood-kin. A Sovereign who grants Wing-Gift to a Blank is committing an act of political treason in most Citadel nations.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, you can target one additional creature per spell level above 3rd.

name: Mist-Form
description: You press an Intact Shadow-Core to a willing creature and speak the Old Tongue dissolution: vrak’sul vish’tal vayr’sul (“flesh to mist to sky”). The creature’s body dissolves into a cloud of dark mist — the color of their bloodline — that can pass through narrow cracks, fly at 10 feet per round, and is resistant to nonmagical damage. They cannot attack, cast spells, or manipulate objects, but they can see and hear dimly. The mist smells faintly of dragon musk. Void-blooded casters find this spell unnervingly comfortable; others find the experience of being bodiless deeply disorienting. The Inquisition sneeringly calls it “the coward’s escape.”

name: Aether-Rune
description: You inscribe a draconic rune onto a surface using an Intact Law-Core as your stylus, weaving a stored spell into the inscription. During the casting, you channel a spell of 3rd level or lower into the rune, which holds it dormant until triggered. Set the trigger conditions — a creature steps on it, speaks a certain word, or opens a warded object. When triggered, the stored spell discharges. If the stored spell is an area effect, it centers on the triggering creature. If it targets a creature, it targets the triggerer. The rune is faintly visible to anyone with active purity; it appears as a glowing draconic character. Aether-Runes are legal for registered Archons to deploy on their own property; laying one in a public space is considered premeditated assault.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, you can store a spell of a level up to the slot used.

name: Quickened Blood
description: You crack an Intact Storm-Core and hurl the pieces at a willing creature while crying the Old Tongue racing-prayer vrak’sul vayr’sul gran! (“blood, fly faster!”). The core’s energy suffuses the target’s bloodstream, and they become a blur of motion. For the duration, the target’s speed doubles, they gain +2 AC, advantage on Dexterity saving throws, and they can take one additional action on their turns — Dash, Disengage, Hide, Use Object, or make a single weapon attack. When the spell ends, the target cannot move or take actions until the end of their next turn as the Quickened Blood recedes, leaving a wave of exhaustion. This is the fastest way to burn out a mortal heart.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, you can target one additional creature per spell level above 3rd.

name: Void’s Maw
description: You tear open a fracture in reality with an Intact Void-Core, speaking the Old Tongue words of the Outer Dark: vel’shath gran, mir’sul sheth’kar (“great emptiness, see us”). A 20-foot-radius sphere of black, star-filled void opens at a point within range. The sphere is filled with the whispers of dead stars, and creatures within are blinded. The void is difficult terrain, and any creature that starts its turn there or enters for the first time on a turn takes 2d6 cold damage. Tentacles of living darkness lash out: any creature ending its turn in the Maw takes an additional 2d6 acid damage (Dexterity save to avoid). This is the signature spell of Void-blooded Penitents, who claim the Maw is the mouth of a slumbering Void-Dragon beyond the stars.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the cold damage increases by 1d6 per spell level above 3rd.

name: Mesmerizing Weave
description: You spin an Intact Mind-Core between your fingers and croon the Old Tongue lullaby of the Dream-Wyrm: sul’nir veyr’sul, mir’thal vish’tal (“still now, eyes to the between”). A 30-foot cube fills with twisting, iridescent patterns — your bloodline sigil, fragmented and multiplied into a hypnotic mandala of impossible colors. Each creature in the area that sees the pattern must make a Wisdom saving throw or become charmed and incapacitated with speed 0 for the duration. The creature stares, transfixed, at the weaving lights. The effect ends if the creature takes damage or is shaken awake. For centuries, Dragon-Lords used this spell to pacify rebellious cities without bloodshed — or so the histories claim.

name: Aether-Sanctuary
description: You plant an Intact Law-Core in the ground and trace a 10-foot circle around it while murmuring the Old Tongue sanctuary prayer: karôs vorthûn, vel’shath mir sul (“a place unbroken, the void sees it not”). A dome of faintly shimmering draconic energy rises, invisible from outside but a warm, safe color from within. The dome keeps out weather, nonmagical projectiles, and any creature not present when cast. The atmosphere inside is dry and comfortable regardless of outside conditions. Up to nine creatures can rest inside. The dome does not block magic, but it contains the smell of your bloodline — a subtle deterrent to most beasts. Nomadic Wild-Bloods and fugitive Penitents both rely on the Aether-Sanctuary to survive the wilds.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the radius increases by 5 feet per spell level above 3rd.

name: Storm-Lance
description: You thrust an Intact Storm-Core forward and scream the Old Tongue word vayr’gran vorn! (“great sky, strike!”). A bolt of draconic lightning 100 feet long and 5 feet wide erupts from your hand, blasting through everything in its path. Each creature in the line must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 lightning damage on failure or half on success. The bolt leaves a scorched trench in the ground and ionizes the air, leaving the smell of ozone and a faint taste of copper in your mouth. Unlike natural lightning, the Storm-Lance curves slightly to hit as many targets as possible — the bloodline remembers how to hunt.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 per spell level above 3rd.

name: Binding Circle
description: You inscribe a 10-foot-radius circle on the ground using an Intact Law-Core, speaking the Old Tongue binding rite: keth’shath sul, vorn’shek mir, vel’kar vorthûn (“hold still, speak none, remain bound”). Silver draconic sigils ignite along the circle’s circumference. Choose aberrations, celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, or undead — the circle wards against that type. Affected creatures cannot enter the circle, attack across it, or target those within. A trapped creature within the circle cannot leave by nonmagical means, has disadvantage on attacks against those outside, and must make a Charisma saving throw to use teleportation. The circle can also invert: trapping something inside instead of keeping it out. This is standard Inquisitor equipment for interrogating captured spellcasters.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the duration increases — 2 hours at 4th, 8 hours at 5th, 24 hours at 7th.

name: Grand Phantasm
description: You hold an Intact Mind-Core before you like a lens and project your imagination into the world, speaking the Old Tongue phrase mir’shek gran, sheth’kar vorn (“great image, become here”). You create an illusion of a creature, object, or terrain feature no larger than a 20-foot cube. The phantasm includes sight, sound, smell, and temperature — a dragon, a burning building, a bottomless chasm. It is so detailed that creatures must spend an action examining it (Investigation check against your spell DC) to discern its nature. Even then, the illusion persists; they simply know it is false. A Grand Phantasm of a dragon will cause real fear; a Grand Phantasm of a Sovereign will cause real deference. The mind believes what the bloodline projects.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the illusion gains permanence at 6th level, requiring no concentration.

name: Healing Roar
description: You crush an Intact Light-Core and roar the Old Tongue rally: vorthûn! vorthûn! (“unbroken! unbroken!”). A wave of golden light bursts from you, washing over up to six creatures you can see. Each target regains hit points equal to 1d4 + your spellcasting ability modifier. The roar carries your draconic intent — it is not a spoken word but a blood-pulse, felt in the chest more than heard. Lore-Keepers who specialize in Healing Roar train their voices for decades, learning to shape the sound to prioritize the most gravely wounded. In the chaos of battle, that roar has pulled more soldiers from death’s edge than any surgeon’s blade.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the healing increases by 1d4 per spell level above 3rd.

name: Aether-Shroud
description: You press an Intact Shadow-Core to a creature’s chest and whisper the Old Tongue phrase veyr’shath mir sul (“eyes see none”). The core’s essence diffuses through the target’s bloodstream, masking their bloodline from all divination magic and scrying sensors. The target cannot be perceived through magical means, cannot be located by spells like Far-Sight or Blood-Seek, and any magical attempt to discern their location, thoughts, or purity simply fails. The shroud is invisible, but the target feels a constant, subtle chill — the shadow of their dragon ancestor wrapping around them like a cloak. Fugitive Archons call this their “second skin.”
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the duration increases to 24 hours at 5th level, 30 days at 8th level.

name: Ghost-Steed
description: You toss an Intact Shadow-Core forward and whistle the Old Tongue steed-call: vayr’vrak sul’dan (“sky-runner, come”). The core unfolds into a quasi-real horse-like creature, dark as shadow with a mane of pale smoke and hooves that strike sparks from empty air. The Ghost-Steed has a speed of 100 feet (or 10 miles per hour at a fast pace), can run across water and quicksand as if solid, and leaves no tracks. It cannot fight and will not enter combat; if you dismount, it waits patiently. When the spell ends, the steed gradually fades over 1 minute, giving the rider time to dismount. Ghost-Steeds are favored by couriers, spies, and anyone who needs to cross the Sump canals without a boat.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the steed’s speed increases by 20 feet per spell level above 3rd.

name: Verdant Surge
description: You bury an Intact Nature-Core in soil and sing the Old Tongue growing-song: vrak’nir gran’sul, sheth’kar vorn (“wild growth, become here”). The core dissolves into the earth, and all plants within a 100-foot radius surge into overgrowth. Grass becomes waist-high, shrubs become walls, and vines wrap every surface. The area becomes difficult terrain for creatures other than you (who can pass unhindered), and any creature that moves through it must spend 4 feet of movement per 1 foot traveled. The plants are mundane but aggressive; if left alone, they produce double yield for one year. This spell is the reason Wild-Blood farmsteads are self-sufficient within weeks. It can restore a blighted field or turn a battlefield into a deathtrap.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the radius increases by 50 feet per spell level above 3rd.

name: Element-Ward
description: You press an Intact Wild-Core against a creature and speak the Old Tongue phrase sheth’sul karôs (“element, be still”). The core shifts color to match the element you choose: red (fire), blue (lightning), white (cold), or green (acid). The target gains resistance to the chosen damage type for the duration. The protection manifests as a faint elemental shimmer — a heat haze around a fire-warded target, a cool mist around a cold-warded target. The magic works by commanding the target’s blood to recognize the element as kin; to a fire-blooded creature, a Cinder Burst is merely a warm breeze. This spell is standard issue for Citadel Purge Squads deploying against elemental-breathing rogue Archons.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, you can target one additional creature per spell level above 3rd.

name: Break the Malediction
description: You press an Intact Law-Core to the cursed creature and intone the Old Tongue severance: vel’shath sheth’kar, vorn’sul mir’shek (“come undone, cursed thing”). The core flares white-hot as it forces the target’s bloodline to reject the foreign magic embedded within. This ends all curses affecting the target — whether from a Wyrm’s Malediction, a cursed item, or another source. The severance is violent; the target takes 1d4 psychic damage as the curse is ripped from their blood, and a visible black mist exudes from their skin before dissipating. The core shatters. Attunement to a cursed item is not broken, but the curse itself is purged. Inquisitors demand that all confiscated cursed items be processed by an Ordained using this spell before storage.

name: Death-Defy
description: You press an Intact Light-Core to the chest of a creature that has died within the last minute and speak the Old Tongue command that no mortal was meant to utter: vrak’sul vorthûn, sheth’kar mir vel’shath! (“flesh unbroken, defy the void!”). The core’s light pours into the corpse, igniting the embers of their bloodline. The creature returns to life with 1 hit point, though they bear a scar where the core touched them — a permanent mark resembling a draconic scale. This spell cannot restore missing body parts or lost purity, nor can it revive someone who died of old age. The Light-Core is consumed in the process. The Inquisition classifies Death-Defy as a restricted miracle — only Ordained of the highest rank may perform it, and every casting must be justified in writing to the Citadel.

name: Far-Speech
description: You hold an Intact Mind-Core to your lips and whisper a message of up to 25 words, followed by the recipient’s full name and bloodline sigil — if you know it. Speak the Old Tongue phrase mir’shek vayr’sul (“words, fly”). The core vibrates, and your words travel along the aetheric current that connects all dragon-blooded creatures, arriving instantly in the recipient’s mind. They hear the message in your voice and can respond immediately with up to 25 words. The connection is one-way per message; hold a conversation by repeated castings. The Mind-Core is not consumed. Messages sent across planar boundaries require an Intact Wild-Core and have a 5% chance of failure per planar boundary crossed.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the message length doubles per spell level above 3rd.

name: Freezing Gale
description: You fling an Intact Frost-Core skyward and shriek the Old Tongue words sul’vayr mir’sul (“freeze the still air”). The core detonates into a 40-foot-radius, 20-foot-high cylinder of driving sleet and razor-sharp ice shards. The area is heavily obscured, and the ground becomes slick (difficult terrain, and any creature that starts its turn there or enters must make a Dexterity save or fall prone). At the start of each of your turns, the storm intensifies, dealing 2d6 cold damage to all creatures within. The flames of any fire in the area are extinguished. Frost-blooded Dragon-Heirs can stand in the heart of their own Freezing Gale and feel only a pleasant chill.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the cold damage increases by 1d6 per spell level above 3rd.

name: Sluggish Blood
description: You point an Intact Frost-Core at up to six creatures in a 40-foot cube and utter the Old Tongue word sul’dan (“slow”). Their blood thickens, their muscles rebel, their draconic spark dims. Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On failure, their speed is halved, they take -2 AC and Dexterity saving throws, and they cannot take reactions. On their turn, they can use either an action or a bonus action — not both — and can make only one attack regardless of Extra Attack. They also have a 25% chance (1 on a d4) to have their spellcasting delayed until their next turn, the aether moving sluggishly through their veins. The target can repeat the save at the end of each turn.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the number of targets increases by two per spell level above 3rd.

name: Corpse-Tongue
description: You press an Intact Death-Core between the jaws of a corpse and speak the Old Tongue question-rite: veyr’shath mir’shek, vrak’sul sul’dan (“dead mouth, speak of what was”). The core turns black and crumbles, and the corpse animates just enough to answer up to five questions — jaw creaking, tongue moving though the lungs are still. The corpse knows only what it knew in life and is not compelled to be truthful (though most are, seeing no reason to lie). Corpses cannot speculate, only recount. Each question you ask burns away more of the remaining spirit-residue; after five, the corpse slumps, unable to speak again even with a new casting. The Inquisition requires a writ from a magistrate to question the dead, though in the Sump, a few silver scales buy the same service.

name: Root-Whisper
description: You kneel and touch an Intact Nature-Core to the ground, humming the Old Tongue greeting that the first dragons used to speak with the forests they claimed as territory: vrak’nir sul’dan, mir’shek vorn (“green things, speak to me here”). For the duration, you can communicate with plants within 30 feet as if they were creatures that understood Old Tongue. You can question them about recent events — a tree remembers who passed beneath it yesterday, a vine recalls what it entangled last night. Plants are not intelligent but are perceptive in their way; they speak of light, water, weight, and movement. The experience is dreamlike. A rose bush cannot describe a face, but it can tell you that something warm and iron-scented passed by at moonrise.

name: Aether-Sentinels
description: You hurl an Intact Law-Core into the air above you and speak the Old Tongue guardian-invocation: keth’nar vorn’sul, sheth’kar mir (“guardians of old, attend this place”). The core splits into drifting motes of light that orbit you in a 15-foot-radius, coalescing momentarily into spectral shapes — dragon-wing silhouettes, scaled fists, roaring maws. When you cast the spell and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you designate creatures in the area: they must make a Wisdom saving throw or take 3d8 radiant damage (half on success). Their speed is halved in the area. The Sentinels are an echo of the Dragon-Lords’ personal guard, spectral remnants that never truly died. Creatures you designate as allies are unaffected.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 per spell level above 3rd.

name: Rot-Breath
description: You crush an Intact Death-Core and exhale the contents into a 20-foot-radius sphere at a point within range, speaking the Old Tongue phrase vrak’sul vel’shath (“flesh-rot, come forth”). The sphere fills with thick, yellow-green fumes that reek of carrion and old dragon’s breath. The area is heavily obscured. Any creature that starts its turn within the cloud must make a Constitution saving throw or spend its action retching and reeling. Creatures that do not need to breathe or are immune to poison automatically succeed. The fumes linger even after the spell ends, the smell clinging to clothing for days. In the Sump, Rot-Breath is called “the dragon’s morning-after” and is used to clear out rival gang strongholds.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the radius increases by 5 feet per spell level above 3rd.

name: All-Tongue
description: You press an Intact Mind-Core to a creature’s throat and speak the Old Tongue phrase mir’shek gran, sheth’kar vorn (“great words, become here”). The core dissolves into the target’s larynx, and for the duration, any creature that knows at least one language can understand everything the target says, regardless of language. The target also understands any spoken language they hear. This is not translation — it is draconic authority imposing comprehension. The words are still spoken in their native tongue, but the meaning is delivered directly to the listener’s mind, accompanied by a faint taste of the target’s bloodline. This spell is essential for diplomatic summits between Houses and the Citadel.

name: Sanguine Grasp
description: You wrap an Intact Blood-Core in your fingers and whisper the Old Tongue thirst-curse: vrak’sul vorn’sul, sul’mir sheth’kar (“your flesh become mine, your life become here”). Your hand becomes wreathed in crimson-black shadow that hungers. Make a melee spell attack against a creature; on a hit, the target takes 3d6 necrotic damage, and you regain hit points equal to half the damage dealt. On each subsequent turn, you can use your action to make the attack again. The shadow-hand does not require contact with bare skin — it phases through armor and clothing to leech directly from the blood beneath. This is the most forbidden spell in Solis-Grave; casting it is an automatic death sentence in every jurisdiction. Even Penitents speak of Sanguine Grasp only in whispers.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 per spell level above 3rd.

name: Gill-Gift
description: You crack an Intact Water-Core and anoint up to ten creatures with its cool, briny essence while speaking the Old Tongue phrase vayr’mir sul’dan (“breathe water, still”). Each target’s neck develops faint scale-like ridges — temporary gills that allow them to breathe underwater for the duration. The gills vanish when the spell ends, leaving no trace. The magic was a gift from the Sea-Dragons to their land-bound allies during the ancient wars, allowing armies to march along seafloors and surprise coastal fortresses. Modern smugglers favor Gill-Gift for moving contraband through flooded Sump tunnels where Inquisitor patrol boats cannot follow.

name: Surface-Tread
description: You toss an Intact Water-Core from creature to creature (up to ten) and speak the Old Tongue command vayr’sul sul’dan (“walk still upon the water”). Each target’s feet shimmer with a faint, fluid light. For the duration, they can move across any liquid surface — water, acid, mud, snow, quicksand, lava — as if it were solid ground. Creatures crossing molten lava still take fire damage from the heat. Targets cannot willingly submerge; the surface refuses them. The magic was originally developed by Dragon-Lords who grew tired of fording rivers on campaign. Strider rangers use Surface-Tread to lead pursuit across lakes and bogs, watching their enemies founder helplessly behind them.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, you can target ten additional creatures per spell level above 3rd.

name: Gale-Barrier
description: You hurl an Intact Storm-Core and split it mid-flight with the Old Tongue words vayr’shul gran (“great wind-wall”). A wall of roaring wind 50 feet long, 15 feet high, and 1 foot thick springs into existence. The wall deflects arrows, bolts, and thrown weapons — they automatically miss anything behind the wall. Gaseous creatures and swarms cannot pass through. Creatures must make a Strength saving throw to move through the wall; on failure, they are pushed back. The wall howls like a dragon in gale-force fury, imposing disadvantage on Perception checks relying on hearing within 30 feet. The wind smells faintly of the caster’s bloodline — an olfactory signature that can identify the caster to those who know the scent.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the wall’s length increases by 25 feet per spell level above 3rd.

name: Boiling Blood
description: You hold an Intact Blood-Core before you and whisper your own name in Old Tongue, then the target’s, binding your bloodlines in painful sympathy: vrak’sul ignar’sul, sheth’vorn vel’shath (“blood boil, I burn so you burn”). Choose a creature you can see; its blood begins to seethe within its veins. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On failure, it takes 3d8 fire damage and is wracked with agony — it has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of its next turn. On subsequent turns, you can use your action to deal 3d8 fire damage automatically. However, each time you do so, you take 1d8 necrotic damage as the hemocraft backlash scorches your own blood. Crimson Oaths consider Boiling Blood their signature curse — a weapon of mutual destruction.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the initial and ongoing damage increases by 1d8 per spell level above 3rd.

name: Clockwork Ward
description: You slot an Intact Law-Core into a palm-sized brass disc inscribed with your maker’s mark and speak the Old Tongue activation phrase keth’nar vorthûn (“guardians, unbroken”). The disc splits into six floating gear-plates that orbit you in a 10-foot radius, continuously rotating. Any hostile creature that enters the radius for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 3d8 slashing damage as the spinning gears tear at them. Allies within the radius are unharmed — the gears recognize your bloodline and your designation of friend from foe. When the spell ends, the gears retract into the disc with a satisfying click. Aether-Smiths refine their Clockwork Wards obsessively, each generation quieter, faster, and more discerning than the last.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 per spell level above 3rd.

name: Bloodline Recall
description: You press an Intact Mind-Core to your temple and roar your own bloodline name in Old Tongue — your full ancestral title, tracing back to the Named Dragon you descend from. The core flashes, and you recover one expended spell slot of 2nd level or lower. This is not simple replenishment; you are reaching back through your bloodline to a moment when that spell was cast by an ancestor, and you are borrowing the unused portion of their power. The experience is disorienting — you briefly relive a fragment of their memory, smell what they smelled, feel what they felt. Bloodline Recall can only be used once per long rest, regardless of how many times it is cast. Dragon-Heirs whisper that overuse risks attracting the attention of ancestors who may not wish to be disturbed.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the recovered spell slot level cap increases by one per spell level above 3rd.

name: Consecrated Ground
description: You kneel and inscribe a 30-foot-radius circle around yourself with an Intact Law-Core, reciting every line of your sworn oath in Old Tongue as you trace. When completed, the circle’s boundary ignites with silver flame, and the ground within becomes consecrated. Allies within the circle gain +2 to AC and saving throws. Any creature that dies within the circle cannot be raised as undead. Any spell of the Necromancy school cast within the circle by a creature other than you requires the caster to succeed on a Spellcasting check against your spell save DC or fail. The consecration is absolute — the flame does not burn, but it rejects the unholy. This is the Oath-Sworn’s ultimate declaration: “This ground is mine. This ground is clean. Nothing profane passes.”
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the radius increases by 10 feet per spell level above 3rd.

name: Trail of the Hunt
description: You crush an Intact Flesh-Core against your chest and inhale deeply, speaking the Old Tongue hunter’s prayer: veyr’shath gran’sul, vrak’sul vorn’nir (“great eyes, show me the blood”). For the duration, you can sense the approximate location, direction, and distance of any creature within 1 mile whose blood you have tasted, whose bloodline sigil you know, or who is currently bleeding. The sense is not visual — it is a pull in your chest, a warmth that grows stronger as you approach, a taste that intensifies on your tongue. You know if the quarry is stationary or moving, and whether their blood-scent is fresh or growing cold. Striders consider Trail of the Hunt sacred; it is taught only to those who have proven they will not abuse the blood-bond between hunter and prey.
higher_level: At 4th level or higher, the range increases by 1 mile per spell level above 3rd.
